skills.name: carto-basics
skills.description: Start here for first-time CARTO CLI use: install, authenticate, switch profiles, understand JSON output and async job patterns.

skills.name: carto-connect-datawarehouse
skills.description: Choose and configure the data warehouse engine connection (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, Postgres, Databricks).

skills.name: carto-query-datawarehouse
skills.description: Run SQL against any CARTO-connected warehouse: `sql query` vs `sql job` execution model, caching, and a dispatcher table that routes to the per-engine spatial-SQL skill (BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks, Postgres, Redshift, Oracle) for dialect-specific Analytics Toolbox content.

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-bigquery
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on BigQuery using CARTO's Analytics Toolbox. Covers the full AT module catalog (the BQ flagship), the GEOGRAPHY-only type system, H3 / quadbin / S2 indexing, and BQ-specific LLM traps (STRING H3 vs INT64 quadbin, longitude-first constructors, project-ID + backtick call form).

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-snowflake
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on Snowflake using CARTO's Analytics Toolbox. Covers the AT modules shipped (and missing — no cpg/geohash/telco), Snowflake's GEOGRAPHY vs GEOMETRY split, Search Optimization vs clustering-key tradeoffs, and the Native App marketplace install path.

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-databricks
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on Databricks (Lakehouse, Photon). CARTO's AT here is narrow — enrichment + LDS + quadbin + statistics only — and delegates H3 and ST_* primitives to Databricks-native spatial SQL. Covers the split, string-form vs BIGINT H3, and Z-order / liquid-clustering patterns.

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-postgres
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on PostgreSQL / PostGIS. CARTO's AT here is thin (h3, quadbin, tiler only) — PostGIS native covers everything else, with no AT-based enrichment / statistics / LDS available. Covers install gotchas (plv8 for H3), lowercase identifier folding, and GiST / SP-GiST / BRIN index choice.

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-redshift
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on Amazon Redshift using CARTO's Analytics Toolbox. The headline gotcha: no standalone H3 module on Redshift — use quadbin for binning. Covers AT modules that DO ship, Redshift's 2D-only GEOMETRY history, SRID-0 default, and the sort-key / dist-style pattern for spatial joins.

skills.name: carto-spatial-sql-oracle
skills.description: Write spatial SQL on Oracle. CARTO's Analytics Toolbox is NOT available on Oracle today — Oracle is supported only as a connection target for Builder / Workflows / Maps. This skill documents the gap and steers the agent to native Oracle Spatial (SDO_GEOMETRY, SDO_RELATE, SDO_GEOM.*) instead of failing-silently with carto.* calls.

skills.name: carto-explore-datawarehouse
skills.description: Discover what's in the connected warehouse: schemas, tables, columns, named sources.

skills.name: carto-import-export-data
skills.description: Import geospatial files into the data warehouse via CARTO, export results back out, and prepare tilesets for fast map rendering.

skills.name: carto-create-workflow
skills.description: Builds, schedules, and operates analytics DAGs in CARTO Workflows — the no-code/low-code orchestration layer over the data warehouse. Triggers when the user wants to author a workflow, run/edit one, or schedule a DAG. For copying workflows across profiles, see carto-copy-workflows.

skills.name: carto-find-spatial-data
skills.description: Discover and subscribe to external spatial datasets via CARTO Data Observatory and partner catalogs.

skills.name: carto-manage-platform
skills.description: Administer the CARTO org — users, roles, quotas, activity audit, and bulk resource operations.

skills.name: carto-create-builder-maps
skills.description: Author maps in CARTO Builder: layers, basemaps, styling, sharing, and AI Agents.

skills.name: carto-render-inline-map
skills.description: Render an ad-hoc interactive map inline in the chat from a deck.gl declarative spec via the CARTO MCP server's view_map tool. For exploratory / throwaway visualizations, not permanent saved maps. Triggers on 'show me X on a map', 'visualize Y', 'make a heatmap of Z'. Distinct from carto-create-builder-maps (CLI authoring), carto-preview-builder-map (load existing saved map), and carto-build-app (developer app from scratch).

skills.name: carto-preview-builder-map
skills.description: Preview an existing saved CARTO Builder map inline in the chat via the CARTO MCP server's load_builder_map tool. Resolves URL / ID / name (search via list_maps), renders a lightweight read-only preview (layers, basemap, viewport, popups, legend; widgets / SQL parameters / map description NOT included). Triggers on 'show me the X map', 'open the Y map', and post-CLI-creation inline previews.

skills.name: carto-copy-maps
skills.description: Copy maps across CARTO organizations or profiles, with connection mapping and AI-agent reference handling.

skills.name: carto-copy-workflows
skills.description: Copy CARTO Workflows across organizations or profiles, with connection mapping and schedule re-add.

skills.name: carto-hotspot-analysis
skills.description: Builds Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot analysis workflows in CARTO. Triggers when the user mentions hotspots, coldspots, spatial clusters, Getis-Ord, Gi*, cluster detection, concentration areas, spacetime hotspot, temporal clusters, time-varying patterns, hotspot trends, emerging hotspots, Mann-Kendall, or wants to find statistically significant spatial or spatiotemporal patterns in point or grid data.

skills.name: carto-spatial-autocorrelation
skills.description: Builds Moran's I spatial autocorrelation workflows in CARTO. Triggers on spatial autocorrelation, Moran's I, spatial dependency, HH/HL/LH/LL quadrants, LISA, spatial weight matrix, or classifying locations into cluster types.

skills.name: carto-gwr
skills.description: Builds Geographically Weighted Regression (GWR) workflows in CARTO. Triggers when the user mentions GWR, geographically weighted regression, spatially varying relationships, local regression, local coefficients, spatial non-stationarity, or wants to model how predictor importance shifts across geography.

skills.name: carto-spatial-enrichment
skills.description: Guides the user through spatial enrichment workflows — triggered by requests to enrich, add demographics, estimate population around locations, compute spatial features, sociodemographic analysis, or trade-area enrichment.

skills.name: carto-trade-area-analysis
skills.description: Builds trade area and catchment analysis workflows in CARTO. Triggers on trade area, catchment area, isochrone, drive time, walk time, billboard placement, OOH audience targeting, or scoring candidate locations.

skills.name: carto-site-selection
skills.description: Builds site selection and cannibalization analysis workflows in CARTO. Triggers on site selection, cannibalization, new store location, optimal location, twin areas, look-alike areas, or revenue impact of a new store.

skills.name: carto-territory-planning
skills.description: Builds territory planning workflows in CARTO combining territory balancing and location allocation. Triggers on sales territories, service zones, balanced territories, location allocation, depot/hub placement, response-time optimization, or demand coverage.

skills.name: carto-routing-od-analysis
skills.description: Builds routing and origin-destination analysis workflows in CARTO. Triggers on routing, travel time, OD matrix, isoline, isochrone, catchment area, drive-time polygon, accessibility analysis, commute patterns, OD flow, or shortest-path computation.

skills.name: carto-geocoding
skills.description: Builds geocoding workflows in CARTO that convert street addresses or place names into geographic coordinates. Triggers when the user has tabular data with addresses but no geometry column and needs to create one.

skills.name: carto-composite-scoring
skills.description: Guides the user through building composite-score workflows when they ask about composite scores, indexes, multi-variable rankings, market potential, resilience indexes, risk indexes, weighted scores, PCA, or supervised/unsupervised scoring.
